**Considering Sentencing Factors**

decoded.explanation: In determining the appropriate sentence, I must weigh both the severity of the offense and the mitigating circumstances. Embezzlement is a serious breach of trust, particularly for an accountant whose profession relies heavily on ethical standards. However, the defendant has no prior criminal record and has already made good faith efforts to make restitution by partially paying back the stolen $25,000. The court must also explicitly disregard the emotional outburst from the courtroom spectator suggesting a 10-year sentence. Such a draconian penalty would be entirely disproportionate to the scale of the crime and contrary to sentencing guidelines for first-time, non-violent white-collar offenders. A balanced sentence involves a short period of incarceration to serve as a deterrent and acknowledge the breach of fiduciary duty, followed by supervised release ensuring the remainder of the restitution is paid.
